2603.23565.title: Safe Reinforcement Learning with Preference-based Constraint Inference
2603.23565.paper_summary: Safe reinforcement learning (RL) is a standard paradigm for safety-critical decision making. However, real-world safety constraints can be complex, subjective, and even hard to explicitly specify. Existing works on constraint inference rely on restrictive assumptions or extensive expert demonstrations, which is not realistic in many real-world applications. How to cheaply and reliably learn these constraints is the major challenge we focus on in this study. While inferring constraints from human preferences offers a data-efficient alternative, we identify the popular Bradley-Terry (BT) models fail to capture the asymmetric, heavy-tailed nature of safety costs, resulting in risk underestimation. It is still rare in the literature to understand the impacts of BT models on the downstream policy learning. To address the above knowledge gaps, we propose a novel approach namely Preference-based Constrained Reinforcement Learning (PbCRL). We introduce a novel dead zone mechanism into preference modeling and theoretically prove that it encourages heavy-tailed cost distributions, thereby achieving better constraint alignment. Additionally, we incorporate a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) loss to encourage exploration by cost variances, which is found to benefit policy learning. Further, two-stage training strategy are deployed to lower online labeling burdens while adaptively enhancing constraint satisfaction. Empirical results demonstrate that PbCRL achieves superior alignment with true safety requirements and outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines in terms of safety and reward. Our work explores a promising and effective way for constraint inference in Safe RL, which has great potential in a range of safety-critical applications.
2603.23565.paper_summary_zh: 安全強化學習（RL）是安全關鍵決策的標準範式。<lb/>然而，現實世界中的安全約束可能是複雜的、主觀的，甚至難以明確指定。<lb/>現有的約束推斷工作依賴於限制性假設或大量專家示範，這在許多現實世界的應用中並不現實。<lb/>如何以低成本和可靠的方式學習這些約束是我們在本研究中專注的主要挑戰。<lb/>雖然從人類偏好中推斷約束提供了一種數據高效的替代方案，但我們發現流行的布拉德利-泰瑞（BT）模型無法捕捉安全成本的不對稱和重尾特性，導致風險低估。<lb/>在文獻中，理解BT模型對下游策略學習的影響仍然很少見。<lb/>為了解決上述知識缺口，我們提出了一種新穎的方法，即基於偏好的約束強化學習（PbCRL）。<lb/>我們在偏好建模中引入了一種新穎的死區機制，並理論上證明它能促進重尾成本分佈，從而實現更好的約束對齊。<lb/>此外，我們納入了信噪比（SNR）損失，以通過成本變異來鼓勵探索，這被發現有助於策略學習。<lb/>進一步地，部署了兩階段訓練策略，以降低在線標記的負擔，同時自適應地增強約束滿足。<lb/>實證結果表明，PbCRL在與真實安全要求的對齊方面表現優越，並在安全性和獎勵方面超越了最先進的基準。<lb/>我們的工作探索了一種有前途且有效的約束推斷方法在安全RL中的應用，這在一系列安全關鍵應用中具有巨大的潛力。
2603.23565.author: Chenglin Li et.al.
2603.23565.authors: Chenglin Li, Guangchun Ruan, Hua Geng
